result:
I think I'd eliminate the worst forms of suffering - not just physical pain, but those deep human agonies: the terror of persecution, the desolation of losing a child, the slow erosion of dignity in extreme poverty. 

This feels like it would open up possibilities for everything else we value. When people aren't trapped in the most devastating circumstances, they can create, discover, connect, and work on improving life in all sorts of other ways.

Of course, even this "simple" change would have incredible complexity - how it would affect human character, resilience, perhaps even meaning itself. Maybe eliminating suffering at that scale would require trade-offs I can't foresee or don't want to contemplate.

But if we're imagining single changes that could make everything else better as a byproduct, that seems worth considering.

What kind of one-change world transformation have you thought about? The question always strikes me as revealing something about what we see as most fundamental to human flourishing.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
